title: fix: Vertex AI multiple incompatibilities via OpenAI-compatible bridge (closes #388)
description:
Fix several Vertex AI bugs in the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions bridge. Issues reported in decolua/9router#388:

1. Vertex URL building produces invalid project/resource paths (RESOURCE_PROJECT_INVALID) — fix URL shape to use projects/{projectId}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/...
2. Streaming requires ?alt=sse query param — add it when streaming is enabled
3. functionCall.id / functionResponse.id fields are unsupported by Vertex — strip them from payloads
4. Tool schema includes unsupported patternProperties — sanitize before sending to Vertex
5. Fake thoughtSignature / reasoning_content injected into Vertex request history causes 400 errors — stop injecting synthetic thought signatures
6. Real Vertex thought signature not preserved across OpenAI-compatible round trips — emit via reasoning_details in assistant tool-call responses, read back when reconstructing Vertex functionCall history, match reasoning_details.id with normalized tool_calls.id

Files to look at: open-sse/services/vertex*.js, open-sse/adapters/vertex*.js, open-sse/translators/vertex*.js (or equivalent Vertex executor files in the codebase).

All fixes should be backwards-compatible.

title: fix: 406 error on /v1/responses endpoint breaks Fabric-AI and other OpenAI clients (closes #389)
description:
Issue decolua/9router#389: When using 9router as an OpenAI-compatible backend with clients like Fabric-AI, POST to /v1/responses returns 406 Not Acceptable. The client sends standard OpenAI chat completions requests to /v1/chat/completions and gets 406 errors.

Investigate: why does /v1/responses return 406 for standard chat completions requests? Check if the route handler validates Content-Type or Accept headers too strictly. The fix should ensure that standard OpenAI clients (sending application/json, expecting application/json) can use 9router as a drop-in backend without 406 errors.
